steps.title: Use the rate of change
steps.content: The coefficient of $x$ is $\frac{9}{5}$, so each $1$-kelvin increase changes the Fahrenheit temperature by $\frac{9}{5}$ degrees.

steps.title: Multiply by the increase
steps.content:
For an increase of $8.40$ kelvins, multiply the kelvin change by the rate.
$$\frac{9}{5}(8.40)$$
The answer is 15.12.
